---
title: "LifespanReflection"
author: "Brian C. Holt, MA"
date: "2026-05-24"
output:
  html_document:
    config:
      search: yes
      toc:
        after: |
          <!-- <li><a href="https://github.com/rstudio/bookdown" target="blank">Published with bookdown</a></li> -->
        before: |
          <li><a href="./">Lifespan Psychology Spring 2026 reflection</a></li>
        scroll_highlight: yes
      toolbar:
        position: fixed
    css: "style.css"
    number_sections: no
    toc: yes
    toc_depth: 6
    toc_float: yes
description: This is a minimal example of using the rmarkdown package for Lifespan
  psychology
link-citations: yes
bibliography: Lifespan.bib
biblio-style: apalike
---

# Purpose

The purpose of this assignment is to review your work across the course, evaluate strengths and weaknesses, identify changes in your thinking, and demonstrate evidence of learning.

This assignment asks you to synthesize and evaluate your work across the semester.

# Important Rule: Portfolio Evidence Cutoff

Only assignments submitted on or before **[insert cutoff date]** may be used as portfolio evidence.

Assignments submitted after this date may still matter for the course, depending on course policy, but they may not be used as evidence in this portfolio reflection.

# Part 0: Course Progress Audit

List your major course assignments.

| Assignment | Status | Date submitted | Eligible for portfolio evidence? |
|:---|:---|:---|:---|
| Assignment 1 | On time / Late / Missing |  | Yes / No |
| Assignment 2 | On time / Late / Missing |  | Yes / No |
| Assignment 3 | On time / Late / Missing |  | Yes / No |
| Assignment 4 | On time / Late / Missing |  | Yes / No |

Then respond:

- Which assignments were completed on time?
- Which assignments were late or missing?
- What patterns do you notice in your course progress?
- What would help you improve your workflow in the future?

# Part 1: Artifact Selection (20%)

From assignments eligible for portfolio evidence, select:

- **2 assignments you consider strongest**
- **1 assignment needing improvement**
- **1 assignment that changed your thinking**

For each provide:

- Assignment title
- Approximate submission date
- 1-2 sentence summary
- Reason for selection

# Part 2: Evidence-Based Evaluation (30%)

Evaluate your work using the intellectual standards:

- Clear
- Accurate
- Precise
- Relevant
- Depth
- Breadth
- Logic
- Significance
- Fairness

For **at least 5 standards**, provide:

- Standard
- Assignment used
- Quoted sentence from original work
- Why it demonstrates or fails to demonstrate this standard
- Possible revision

# Part 3: Revision Exercise (20%)

Choose **one sentence or paragraph** from earlier eligible work.

Provide:

- Original text
- Revised text
- Explanation of change

Possible prompts:

- Did your evidence improve?
- Did your thinking become more precise?
- Did you become more skeptical?
- Did course material alter your interpretation?

# Part 4: Growth Despite Incomplete Work

If you have missing or late assignments, respond to the following:

- Identify one assignment that was missing or late.
- What made that assignment difficult to complete on time?
- What learning, practice, or feedback did you miss by not completing it on time?
- What would you do differently in a future course?

If you do not have missing or late assignments, respond to this instead:

- What helped you stay current with the work?
- What habit or strategy would you recommend to another student?

# Part 5: Reflection and Growth (20%)

Respond to the following:

## Question 1

What idea from the course most changed your thinking?

## Question 2

What misconception did you have earlier in the semester?

## Question 3

Which assignment best represents your learning?

## Question 4

What would you improve if you repeated the course?

# Part 6: AI Statement (10%)

AI use is permitted.

If AI tools were used, describe:

- Where used
- Purpose
- Whether helpful
- Limitations encountered

If AI tools were not used:

- State that no AI tools were used

# Evaluation Rubric

| Category | Points |
|:---|---:|
| Course progress audit | 10 |
| Artifact selection quality | 15 |
| Evidence use | 25 |
| Revision quality | 20 |
| Reflection depth | 20 |
| AI disclosure | 10 |
| **Total** | **100** |

# Notes

- Students are encouraged to reuse prior eligible work.
- No new outside research is required.
- The goal is synthesis, evaluation, and reflection rather than creating a new research paper.
- Late or missing work should be discussed honestly as part of the learning process.
